questions.q: A coil of insulated copper wire is connected to a sensitive galvanometer. If a bar magnet is moved towards the coil, then which deflection will be observed in the galvanometer?
questions.answer: A deflection will be observed in the galvanometer, as the changing magnetic field induces an electromotive force.

questions.q: If a coil of 100 turns and a magnetic flux of 5 × 10^-3 Wb is linked with it, then the induced electromotive force is (e = -N(dφ/dt))
A coil of 100 turns is kept near a bar magnet. The magnetic flux linked with the coil is given by φ = 2t^2 + 3t + 1, where t is in seconds. The emf induced in the coil is
Induction of an electric field in a conductor is due to _______
The phenomenon of electromagnetic induction was discovered by _______
A coil of 100 turns is connected to a galvanometer. The magnetic flux linked with the coil is given by φ = 2t^2 + 3t + 1, where t is in seconds. The deflection in the galvanometer is
questions.answer: The deflection in the galvanometer is due to the induced emf, which is given by e = -N(dφ/dt).

questions.q: The rate of change of magnetic flux linked with a coil is 10^-3 Wb/s. The induced emf in the coil is
A coil of 100 turns is connected to a galvanometer. The magnetic flux linked with the coil is φ = 2t^2 + 3t + 1, where t is in seconds. The emf induced in the coil is
The phenomenon of electromagnetic induction is used in _______
A coil of 100 turns is kept near a bar magnet. The magnetic flux linked with the coil is given by φ = 2t^2 + 3t + 1, where t is in seconds. The induced emf in the coil is
questions.answer: The emf induced in the coil is given by e = -N(dφ/dt).
